strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 appends the new route after the existing GET /items/:id handler, keeping the two /items GET routes (list and detail) contiguous, while P2 inserts POST /items/import between GET /items and GET /items/:id, splitting the paired handlers.

P1 computes `nextId` with `Math.max(0, ...items.map((item) => item.id)) + 1`, which spreads the whole items array as call arguments (risking a stack-size RangeError on large arrays) and silently produces NaN ids if any existing item.id is non-numeric, whereas P2's `items.reduce(...)` with an explicit typeof/Number.isFinite guard avoids both failure modes.

P1 adds a third test, 'POST /items/import rejects a non-array items body without changes', explicitly exercising the spec's required invalid_body 400 branch, while P2's two tests never cover that branch, leaving it unguarded against regression.

P2 destructures `const { items: batch } = req.body || {}` once and reuses `batch`/`importedItems` consistently, while P1 repeats `req.body.items` four separate times across the handler, making it more verbose and error-prone to maintain.
